title: Electromagnetic Waves: Understanding the Basics
summary: Electromagnetic waves are a form of energy that travel through space at the speed of light, which is vital for various technologies and understanding physics. These invisible carriers allow us to communicate over vast distances.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: Electromagnetic waves are ripples in the electromagnetic field that can travel through a vacuum or material medium without requiring any physical substance for their propagation.
key_points: They consist of oscillating electric and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other
Can carry energy from one place to another

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: Electromagnetic waves are produced by the acceleration of charged particles, like electrons. The changing electric field generates a varying magnetic field and vice versa.
key_points: They propagate at light speed in vacuum according to Maxwell's equations
Can vary widely across different parts of the electromagnetic spectrum

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Electromagnetic waves are fundamental for modern communication, medical imaging techniques such as MRI and X-rays, and they help us understand astronomical objects.
key_points: They form the basis of wireless communications like radio, television, and cellphones
Crucial in diagnostic tools for non-invasive medical examination

key_facts: Electromagnetic waves can travel through a vacuum.
